<@393585313618067456> does polux need WoD
Gimme a sec
Pollux’s WoD is generally good for any Dps
Not just Pollux
Its main stat is Crit Rate, which is really nice(unless your endgame with built Cov and Team, and WoDs), and gives Base Dmg and Crit Dmg increase just- straight up.

It’s “passive” is more of a weird case that can be nice, but hard to use effectively with the start of turn effect. However the “On Exalt” effect can be planned around, same as the Ultra Turn double effect “Ablaze”.
Bit complex, but relatively good

I’m not sure if I told you yet, but if I have, excuse me repeating myself.

There is a mode called D-Tide, where you want 5 separate built teams to clear. You can use a support awakener once per D-Tide. Using a maxed lvl Awakener support can carry one of your 5 teams, leaving you with 4 other teams to build. You can only use each Awakener and WoDs once per D-Tide cycle. You want to clear to 325 as quick as possible, as what you want is time gated. The material is a endgame mat that allows you to limit break your character from Lvl 60 to Lvl 70. Each D-Tide cycle is 2 weeks.
The minimum, Alert 1 difficulty, is Lvl 40

Ogier has a little complex kit, read the fine print of his passive, and you’ll be fine
He gains Fortress which reduces ALL dmg, poison, corossion(Castor), brute damage

Eh~
The shield is meh because Castor’s Corrosion multiplies your dmg too much(broken ah unit)
The problem is Fortress
Which does the opposite
Cutting your dmg down by a %
(Including Corrosion)

Oh yea, furthermore, in D-Tide, every cycle you get a ring of a specific Realm, this time being Chaos. The ring gives a FREE potrait(passive) of Awakeners in that realm. In the manner, all Awakeners who are in the realm of the ring are much stronger this cycle.
You can see what I mean, via this cycle being Chaos. Tawil, Hameln, Ogier team versus Castor, Clem, Jenkin.
It kind of depends on the boss, and changes every D-Tide. This D-Tide, I’ve seen people borrow a Maxed out Kathuga-Ra and clear Alert 4, Wave 1(Or was it 2)
With her as carry
